comment.name: Comment
comment.text:
Afternoon Report

Last Updated: 3/2/1:22PM


It has been nonstop blue skies and sunshine today, which has been an absolute treat. Days like this are a good reminder of just how spectacular our surroundings really are. It’s the kind of scenery that stops you mid-run and makes you take it all in.

The afternoon has warmed up a touch, and with the right layers on, conditions out there are nothing short of beautiful. And the good news is that there is still plenty of time to get out and make the most of it.

On the grooming front, the team will be running through nearly the same lineup as last night with one adjustment. They'll be giving upper 3D a touch up as well.

We also have one eye on tomorrow night's forecast, where some potential snow showers are tracking our way. We'll keep you posted as that develops, but for now, the mountain is yours to enjoy.

We will see you out there.

-Team Snow

comment.name: Comment 2
comment.text:
Good morning, Sunday River!

It is a beautiful day to be out on the mountain, and with sunshine overhead, the miles of views up top are going to be tough to beat.

Yes, it is a cold one, and we may see a little wind drifting through at times, but this is good, honest ski weather. Add an extra layer, make sure everything is zipped up tight, and you will be good to go for lifts spinning at 9AM. And when you are ready for a warm and comfortable ride back up, both the Jordan 8 and Barker 6 have you covered. Those bubbles make days like today extra sweet.

Our grooming team has been out making a couple of passes on terrain across the mountain to set things up for the day. Expect a firmer snow surface than we have seen recently, especially on those first runs of the morning.

Looking ahead, there is snow in the forecast for tomorrow evening. Nothing major, but enough for a nice little refresh across the mountains, so we will take it.

Bundle up, enjoy the sunshine, and we will see you out there.

-Team Snow
